phrase.text: Interviewer - Why was the ISPS developed?

Captain James – According to The International Maritime Organisation (<IMO>) "The International Ship and Port Facility Security Code (ISPS Code) is a <comprehensive set of measures> to enhance the <security of ships> and port facilities, developed in response to the <perceived threats> to ships and port facilities in the wake of the 9/11 attacks in the United States".
Interviewer – I understand that the development and implementation of the ISPS Code was speeded up, why was that?

Captain James - Development and implementation were speeded up considerably in reaction to the <September 11, 2001 attacks> and the <bombing> of the French oil tanker the Limburg. The U.S. Coast Guard, as the lead agency in the United States delegation to the International Maritime Organization (the IMO), advocated for this.
